mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative return distributions with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields no statistically significant outperformance over single-series signals, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of co-occurring expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which correlates with higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases during expansion regimes, or the mechanism reverses direction during contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend levels confirms robust aggregate demand, which supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5% annually forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 55% of threshold-crossing events, or the inflation signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL remains above 2.5% for consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending capacity and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate positive alpha, or 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods where both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints and reduced corporate output, triggering immediate sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal shows no directional consistency across tightening periods.
